_meta.experiment: #53 Paper G reinforcement — DENSE depth curve of SAE feature absorption, Spectra twins (TriLM ternary vs FloatLM FP)

models.note: true twins: LLaMA 30L d=2304 vocab=50304, same data/300B tokens, differ only in precision

env_training_SAEs.note: SAE training ran before the sae-bench downgrade

_meta.method: BatchTopK SAE (d_sae=6.4x d_in, k=32, 4000 steps, 400k acts) per layer; SAEBench feature-absorption over HookedTransformer-shim over HF

verdict: REFINES the 3-point result (honest self-correction). ROBUST contrast: at LATE depth (L24-28) ternary absorbs 1.6-3x MORE than its FP twin (0.28/0.27 vs 0.10/0.17); at the ternary trough (L16) it absorbs ~2x LESS (0.093 vs 0.193). Confound STILL removed (ternary-vs-FP controlled twins). CORRECTION: the 3-point story 'ternary non-monotonic V vs FP monotone' is FALSE at higher resolution — the FP twin is ALSO non-monotonic (trough at L24). Truer statement: BOTH have depth structure but DIFFERENT (ternary trough earlier L16 + higher late; FP trough later L24 + lower late) => the absorption depth-profile depends on precision. The dense curve AVOIDED an overclaim the 3 points would have made. Caveats: 1 seed, untuned BatchTopK SAEs, run under saturated CPU/VRAM (deterministic per-process, unaffected).
